data.title: As a developer, I can rely on fix-bug Gate 1a to pause investigation when intent-search detects conflicting caller usage
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Modify fix-bug SKILL.md Step 1.5 (Gate 1a) to handle the new INTENT_CONFLICT signal from the enhanced intent-search agent. When INTENT_CONFLICT is emitted, investigation pauses and the user is prompted with three options.
**Why**: The enhanced intent-search can now detect when callers depend on the behavior the ticket wants to change, but fix-bug needs to know how to handle this new signal — pausing investigation and routing to the user with evidence.
**Scope**:
- IN: Modify plugins/dso/skills/fix-bug/SKILL.md Step 1.5 to add INTENT_CONFLICT handling alongside existing intent-aligned/intent-contradicting/ambiguous outcomes, user prompt with 3 options (confirm ticket correct → proceed, confirm current behavior correct → close ticket, revise ticket description → update and re-run), investigation pause until user responds, backward-compatible with existing signal parsing
- OUT: Intent-search agent changes themselves (Story 3), scope-drift reviewer (Stories 1-2)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, fix-bug Step 1.5 recognizes the INTENT_CONFLICT signal from intent-search and pauses investigation, presenting the user with caller evidence and three resolution options
  ← Satisfies: "The user is prompted to confirm or revise the ticket before investigation proceeds"
- When this story is complete, selecting "confirm ticket correct" proceeds to investigation (Step 2), "confirm current behavior correct" closes the ticket, and "revise ticket description" allows the user to update the ticket before re-evaluation
  ← Satisfies: "When a bug ticket contains an incorrect behavioral assumption, investigation pauses"
- When this story is complete, the INTENT_CONFLICT signal is additive alongside existing Gate 1a signal types — existing intent-aligned, intent-contradicting, and ambiguous handling remains unchanged
  ← Satisfies: "Existing Gate 1a/1b/2a-2d signal contracts remain unchanged"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Schema backward compatibility — INTENT_CONFLICT must not break existing Gate 1a parsing; treat as a new terminal outcome alongside the existing three
- [Testing] Test the three user resolution paths with synthetic INTENT_CONFLICT signals

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
